name: execute_sql
label: Execute SQL
description: Run SQL on the user's local omi.db SQLite database. Use for app usage stats, screen time, activity counts, task lookups, aggregations. Read-only in agent adapters.
promptSnippet: execute_sql - Query the user's local omi.db SQLite database (SELECT only)
promptGuidelines: Use execute_sql for quantitative queries (counts, sums, date ranges, aggregations).
Use semantic_search instead for fuzzy or conceptual queries about screen content.
runtimePreconditions: SELECT-only in ask-mode and local-agent API projections.

capabilityDoc.title: Execute SQL
capabilityDoc.summary: Run SQL on the local omi.db database for structured local data.
bullets: Supports SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE.
Use for personal facts, app usage stats, time queries, task lookups, conversations, memories, aggregations, and anything structured.
Supports FTS5 MATCH queries for keyword search; see the schema footer for FTS tables and patterns.
SELECT queries auto-limit to 200 rows. UPDATE/DELETE require WHERE. DROP/ALTER/CREATE are blocked.
Prefer semantic_search for fuzzy screen-history questions and backend task tools for creating/updating tasks.

name: semantic_search
label: Semantic Search
description: Vector similarity search on the user's screen history. Use for fuzzy/conceptual queries about what the user saw on their computer.
promptSnippet: semantic_search - Search screen history by meaning
promptGuidelines: Prefer semantic_search over execute_sql when the user asks about something they 'saw' or worked on.
runtimePreconditions: Requires local Rewind screen-history data.

capabilityDoc.title: Semantic Search
capabilityDoc.summary: Vector similarity search on the user's screen history.
bullets: Use for fuzzy/conceptual questions about what the user saw, read, or worked on where exact SQL keywords will not work.
Examples: "reading about machine learning", "working on design mockups".
Parameters: query (required), days (default 7), app_filter (optional).

search_screen_history.title: Search Screen History
search_screen_history.summary: Search the user's on-screen history by meaning.
bullets: Use for what the user saw, read, or worked on. Speak a short summary of the result.

voice.realtimeDescription: Search the user's on-screen history — what they saw, read, or worked on — by meaning. Use for 'when was I looking at X', 'find where I read about Y', 'what was I doing in app Z'. Returns matching moments with the app and context. Fast synchronous read. Speak the result.

name: get_daily_recap
label: Daily Recap
description: Pre-formatted daily activity recap: app usage, conversations, tasks, focus, memories, observations.
promptSnippet: get_daily_recap - Get a daily activity summary
runtimePreconditions: Requires local Omi activity data.

capabilityDoc.title: Daily Recap
capabilityDoc.summary: Pre-formatted activity recap: apps, conversations, tasks, focus, memories, and observations.
bullets: Use for what the user did today/yesterday/this week; it is faster than composing many SQL queries.
Parameters: days_ago (0=today, 1=yesterday, 7=past week; default 1).

voice.realtimeDescription: Get a recap of what the user actually DID on their Mac — apps used (with minutes), conversations, tasks, focus sessions, and screen activity — for a day. First choice for 'what did I do yesterday', 'what did I do today', 'which apps did I use the most', 'how did I spend my time': one fast synchronous read, where searching conversations or spawning an agent would be slower and less complete. Speak a short summary of what it returns.

name: fill_cloud_connector_form
label: Fill Cloud Connector Form
description: Fill the currently visible ChatGPT or Claude custom MCP connector form using Omi's native macOS Accessibility automation. Use first for one-click cloud connector setup after opening the signed-in browser to the connector page.
promptSnippet: fill_cloud_connector_form - Fill and optionally submit the visible ChatGPT/Claude MCP connector form
promptGuidelines: Call this first for ChatGPT or Claude cloud MCP connector setup when the connector form is visible.
Do not install browser extensions before trying this tool.
If it reports missing Accessibility permission, missing form, or missing required fields, wait for the missing condition or use guarded screenshots before any keyboard automation.

provider.description: Cloud platform whose connector form is visible.

server_url.description: Remote MCP server URL to paste into the connector form.

oauth_client_id.description: OAuth Client ID. Defaults to Omi's public ChatGPT/Claude connector client.

oauth_client_secret.description: OAuth Client Secret, only for confidential clients.

token_auth_method.description: OAuth token auth method. Use 'none' for Omi's public ChatGPT connector client.

auth_url.description: OAuth authorization URL when the form asks for it.

token_url.description: OAuth token URL when the form asks for it.

submit.description: Whether to press the visible Add/Connect/Create button after filling required fields.

runtimePreconditions: Requires a signed-in supported browser on the connector page.
Requires macOS Accessibility permission for Omi and the target browser.

capabilityDoc.title: Fill Cloud Connector Form
capabilityDoc.summary: Fill the visible ChatGPT or Claude custom MCP connector form using Omi's native macOS Accessibility automation.
bullets: Call this first for ChatGPT or Claude cloud MCP connector setup when the connector form is visible.
Do not install browser extensions before trying this tool.

name: list_agent_sessions
label: List Agent Sessions
description:
List Omi-managed agent sessions from the local runtime kernel.

Use when the user asks what Omi agents/subagents are active, recent, failed, or attached to a surface.
Returns canonical session/run summaries plus task_agents and floating_agent_pills projections.
promptSnippet: list_agent_sessions - List Omi-managed agent sessions and active runs
promptGuidelines: Use for current or recent kernel-backed Omi agents/subagents across chat, PTT/realtime, task chat, and floating-bar pills.
Returns task_agents and floating_agent_pills alongside canonical session summaries.

ownerId.description: Owner id to list. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

surfaceKind.description: Filter to a canonical surface kind.

limit.description: Maximum sessions to return. Default 50, max 200.

beforeUpdatedAtMs.description: Pagination cursor: only sessions updated before this epoch-ms timestamp.

ownerId.description: Owner id to list. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

surfaceKind.description: Filter to a canonical surface kind.

limit.description: Maximum sessions to return. Default 50, max 200.

beforeUpdatedAtMs.description: Pagination cursor: only sessions updated before this epoch-ms timestamp.

capabilityDoc.title: List Agent Sessions
capabilityDoc.summary: List Omi-managed agent sessions from the local runtime kernel.
bullets: Use for current or recent kernel-backed Omi agents/subagents across chat, PTT/realtime, task chat, and floating-bar pills.
Returns task_agents and floating_agent_pills alongside canonical session summaries.

voice.realtimeDescription: List canonical Omi-managed agent sessions/runs across chat, PTT/realtime, task chat, floating-bar pills, and migrated surfaces. Use when the user asks what canonical agents or subagents are active, recent, failed, or manageable.

runtimePreconditions: Defaults ownerId to the active signed-in owner when omitted.

name: get_agent_run
label: Get Agent Run
description:
Inspect one canonical Omi agent run.

Use a runId returned by list_agent_sessions or a correlated Omi response. Returns the run, session, attempts, adapter bindings, artifact metadata, and optionally events.
promptSnippet: get_agent_run - Inspect one Omi agent run
promptGuidelines: Use a runId from list_agent_sessions or a correlated Omi result.
Returns the run, attempts, adapter bindings, events, and artifact metadata.

ownerId.description: Owner guard. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

includeEvents.description: Include ordered kernel events. Default true.

eventLimit.description: Maximum events to return. Default 100, max 500.

ownerId.description: Owner guard. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

includeEvents.description: Include ordered kernel events. Default true.

eventLimit.description: Maximum events to return. Default 100, max 500.

capabilityDoc.title: Get Agent Run
capabilityDoc.summary: Inspect one canonical Omi agent run.
bullets: Use a runId from list_agent_sessions or a correlated Omi result.
Returns the run, attempts, adapter bindings, events, and artifact metadata.

voice.realtimeDescription: Inspect one canonical Omi-managed agent run. Prefer an agentRef from list_agent_sessions.

includeEvents.description: Include ordered kernel events. Default true.

runtimePreconditions: Requires a canonical Omi run_id.
Defaults ownerId to the active signed-in owner when omitted and rejects runs outside that owner.

name: build_desktop_awareness_snapshot
label: Build Desktop Awareness Snapshot
description: Build a local coordinator snapshot from kernel sessions, runs, dispatches, deliveries, candidates, and runtime health.
promptSnippet: build_desktop_awareness_snapshot - Inspect local coordinator state
promptGuidelines: Use before routing new local work or summarizing open agent loops.
Returns metadata and local state summaries, not raw transcripts or screenshot bytes.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to active owner.

limit.description: Maximum rows per collection. Default 50, max 200.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to active owner.

limit.description: Maximum rows per collection. Default 50, max 200.

capabilityDoc.title: Build Desktop Awareness Snapshot
capabilityDoc.summary: Build a local coordinator snapshot from kernel sessions, runs, dispatches, deliveries, candidates, and runtime health.
bullets: Use before routing new local work or summarizing open agent loops.
Returns metadata and local state summaries, not raw transcripts or screenshot bytes.

runtimePreconditions: Defaults ownerId to the active signed-in owner when omitted.

name: list_desktop_action_queue
label: List Desktop Action Queue
description: Return the derived Desktop action queue from runs, dispatches, deliveries, candidates, legacy projections, and overrides.
promptSnippet: list_desktop_action_queue - List pending local agent attention items
promptGuidelines: Use for approvals, failed runs, artifact review, stale work, and candidate review.
The queue is derived and not persisted as authority.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to active owner.

staleAfterMs.description: Age after which active runs are considered stale.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to active owner.

staleAfterMs.description: Age after which active runs are considered stale.

capabilityDoc.title: List Desktop Action Queue
capabilityDoc.summary: Return the derived Desktop action queue from runs, dispatches, deliveries, candidates, legacy projections, and overrides.
bullets: Use for approvals, failed runs, artifact review, stale work, and candidate review.
The queue is derived and not persisted as authority.

runtimePreconditions: Defaults ownerId to active owner. Does not persist queue rows.

name: get_desktop_open_loops
label: Get Desktop Open Loops
description: Summarize unresolved local coordinator loops: blocking dispatches, failed/stale runs, undelivered artifacts, and candidate reviews.
promptSnippet: get_desktop_open_loops - Summarize unresolved local agent work
promptGuidelines: Use for quick status answers and voice status summaries.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to active owner.
Owner id. Defaults to active owner.

capabilityDoc.title: Get Desktop Open Loops
capabilityDoc.summary: Summarize unresolved local coordinator loops: blocking dispatches, failed/stale runs, undelivered artifacts, and candidate reviews.
bullets: Use for quick status answers and voice status summaries.

runtimePreconditions: Defaults ownerId to active owner.

name: build_desktop_context_packet
label: Build Desktop Context Packet
description: Persist a minimized DesktopContextPacket plus context-access audit rows from explicit selected snippets.
promptSnippet: build_desktop_context_packet - Build scoped context for a local worker run
promptGuidelines: Use selected snippets with provenance, not full transcripts or screenshot image bytes.
Requires a positive TTL and writes context-access audit rows.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to active owner.

surfaceKind.description: Surface kind such as main_chat or task_chat.

packetJson.description: Selected context snippets and policy fields.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to active owner.

surfaceKind.description: Surface kind such as main_chat or task_chat.

packetJson.description: Selected context snippets and policy fields.

capabilityDoc.title: Build Desktop Context Packet
capabilityDoc.summary: Persist a minimized DesktopContextPacket plus context-access audit rows from explicit selected snippets.
bullets: Use selected snippets with provenance, not full transcripts or screenshot image bytes.
Requires a positive TTL and writes context-access audit rows.

runtimePreconditions: Rejects missing TTL and raw screenshot image bytes.

name: route_desktop_intent
label: Route Desktop Intent
description: Run deterministic local intent routing over action queue and reusable session candidates.
promptSnippet: route_desktop_intent - Decide quick answer, resume, fork, delegate, dispatch, or new run
promptGuidelines: Use before creating a new run when existing local context may be relevant.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to active owner.
Owner id. Defaults to active owner.

capabilityDoc.title: Route Desktop Intent
capabilityDoc.summary: Run deterministic local intent routing over action queue and reusable session candidates.
bullets: Use before creating a new run when existing local context may be relevant.

runtimePreconditions: Uses deterministic rules and returns an explanation.

name: evaluate_desktop_tool_policy
label: Evaluate Desktop Tool Policy
description: Evaluate local coordinator policy for a tool/capability request without executing the tool.
promptSnippet: evaluate_desktop_tool_policy - Check local capability policy
promptGuidelines: Use to explain why a sensitive local action needs dispatch or approval.

requestedBundles.description: Optional explicit capability bundles being requested.
Optional explicit capability bundles being requested.

capabilityDoc.title: Evaluate Desktop Tool Policy
capabilityDoc.summary: Evaluate local coordinator policy for a tool/capability request without executing the tool.
bullets: Use to explain why a sensitive local action needs dispatch or approval.

runtimePreconditions: Does not create grants or execute tools.

name: create_desktop_dispatch
label: Create Desktop Dispatch
description: Create a durable local DesktopCoordinatorDispatch for approvals, routing choices, artifact review, candidates, or sensitive context.
promptSnippet: create_desktop_dispatch - Create a durable local decision item
promptGuidelines: Use when user attention or approval is required before crossing a boundary.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to active owner.

sourceSessionId.description: Optional source Omi session_id scope guard.

sourceRunId.description: Optional source Omi run_id scope guard.

sourceAttemptId.description: Optional source Omi attempt_id scope guard.

sourceArtifactId.description: Optional source Omi artifact_id scope guard.

expiresAtMs.description: Optional epoch-ms expiration for the decision item.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to active owner.

sourceSessionId.description: Optional source Omi session_id scope guard.

sourceRunId.description: Optional source Omi run_id scope guard.

sourceAttemptId.description: Optional source Omi attempt_id scope guard.

sourceArtifactId.description: Optional source Omi artifact_id scope guard.

expiresAtMs.description: Optional epoch-ms expiration for the decision item.

capabilityDoc.title: Create Desktop Dispatch
capabilityDoc.summary: Create a durable local DesktopCoordinatorDispatch for approvals, routing choices, artifact review, candidates, or sensitive context.
bullets: Use when user attention or approval is required before crossing a boundary.

runtimePreconditions: Defaults ownerId to active owner. Source refs must match owner scope.

name: resolve_desktop_dispatch
label: Resolve Desktop Dispatch
description: Resolve or cancel a pending local DesktopCoordinatorDispatch, optionally creating a scoped allow grant for an explicit approval.
promptSnippet: resolve_desktop_dispatch - Resolve a durable local decision item
promptGuidelines: Use only for explicit user approval/denial/cancel decisions.

ownerId.description: Owner guard. Defaults to active owner.

grant.description: Optional scoped grant to create for an explicit allow resolution.

ownerId.description: Owner guard. Defaults to active owner.

grant.description: Optional scoped grant to create for an explicit allow resolution.

capabilityDoc.title: Resolve Desktop Dispatch
capabilityDoc.summary: Resolve or cancel a pending local DesktopCoordinatorDispatch, optionally creating a scoped allow grant for an explicit approval.
bullets: Use only for explicit user approval/denial/cancel decisions.

runtimePreconditions: Defaults ownerId to active owner and refuses expired dispatches.
When grant is supplied for a resolved approval, grant creation and approval.resolved event append happen in one transaction.

name: cancel_agent_run
label: Cancel Agent Run
description:
Request cancellation for one canonical Omi agent run through the runtime kernel.

Use when the user asks to stop a running Omi agent/subagent. Returns whether cancellation was accepted, dispatched to the adapter, and acknowledged by the adapter.
promptSnippet: cancel_agent_run - Stop a running Omi agent
promptGuidelines: Use when the user asks to stop a running Omi agent/subagent.
Returns whether cancellation was accepted, dispatched, and acknowledged.

ownerId.description: Owner guard. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.
Owner guard. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

capabilityDoc.title: Cancel Agent Run
capabilityDoc.summary: Request cancellation for one canonical Omi agent run through the runtime kernel.
bullets: Use when the user asks to stop a running Omi agent/subagent.
Returns whether cancellation was accepted, dispatched, and acknowledged.

voice.realtimeDescription: Request cancellation for one canonical Omi-managed agent run. Use when the user asks to stop or kill a running canonical agent/subagent.

runId.description: Canonical Omi run id to cancel.

runtimePreconditions: Requires a canonical Omi run_id.
Defaults ownerId to the active signed-in owner when omitted and rejects runs outside that owner.

name: inspect_agent_artifacts
label: Inspect Agent Artifacts
description:
Inspect canonical artifact metadata for an Omi agent artifact, session, run, or attempt.

Returns metadata and references only. It does not read arbitrary artifact contents.
promptSnippet: inspect_agent_artifacts - Inspect Omi agent artifact metadata
promptGuidelines: Returns artifact references and metadata only.
Use after get_agent_run when the user asks what files or outputs an agent produced.

ownerId.description: Owner guard. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

limit.description: Maximum artifacts to return. Default 50, max 200.

ownerId.description: Owner guard. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

limit.description: Maximum artifacts to return. Default 50, max 200.

capabilityDoc.title: Inspect Agent Artifacts
capabilityDoc.summary: Inspect canonical artifact metadata for an Omi agent artifact, session, run, or attempt.
bullets: Returns artifact references and metadata only.
Use after get_agent_run when the user asks what files or outputs an agent produced.

voice.realtimeDescription: Inspect metadata and references for canonical Omi-managed agent artifacts. Does not read arbitrary artifact contents.

runtimePreconditions: Requires at least one of artifactId, sessionId, runId, or attemptId.
Defaults ownerId to the active signed-in owner when omitted and rejects selectors outside that owner.

name: update_agent_artifact_lifecycle
label: Update Agent Artifact Lifecycle
description:
Update metadata-only lifecycle state for one canonical Omi agent artifact.

This only records artifact metadata state and ordered kernel events. It does not open files, delete files, retain blobs, or read artifact contents.
promptSnippet: update_agent_artifact_lifecycle - Mark an Omi agent artifact retained, dismissed, or opened
promptGuidelines: Use to mark artifact metadata as retained, dismissed, or opened after a user-visible artifact decision.
Pass sessionId, runId, or attemptId when available as a scope guard.
This never reads artifact contents and has no OS side effects.

sessionId.description: Optional canonical Omi session_id scope guard.

runId.description: Optional canonical Omi run_id scope guard.

attemptId.description: Optional canonical Omi attempt_id scope guard.

ownerId.description: Owner guard. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

reason.description: Optional short reason for the lifecycle event.

metadata.description: Small structured metadata for the lifecycle event.

sessionId.description: Optional canonical Omi session_id scope guard.

runId.description: Optional canonical Omi run_id scope guard.

attemptId.description: Optional canonical Omi attempt_id scope guard.

ownerId.description: Owner guard. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

reason.description: Optional short reason for the lifecycle event.

metadata.description: Small structured metadata for the lifecycle event.

capabilityDoc.title: Update Agent Artifact Lifecycle
capabilityDoc.summary: Update metadata-only lifecycle state for one canonical Omi agent artifact.
bullets: Use to mark artifact metadata as retained, dismissed, or opened after a user-visible artifact decision.
Pass sessionId, runId, or attemptId when available as a scope guard.
This never reads artifact contents and has no OS side effects.

voice.realtimeDescription: Update metadata-only lifecycle state for one canonical Omi-managed agent artifact. Does not open, delete, retain, or read files.

sessionId.description: Optional canonical Omi session id scope guard.

runId.description: Optional canonical Omi run id scope guard.

attemptId.description: Optional canonical Omi attempt id scope guard.

runtimePreconditions: Requires artifactId.
Defaults ownerId to the active signed-in owner when omitted and rejects artifacts outside that owner.
Optional sessionId, runId, and attemptId must match the artifact scope.

name: send_agent_message
label: Send Agent Message
description:
Send a follow-up message to an existing canonical Omi agent session.

Creates a new run in that session through the runtime kernel.
promptSnippet: send_agent_message - Continue an Omi-managed agent session
promptGuidelines: Use when continuing a multi-turn conversation with an Omi-managed agent by sessionId.
Creates a new run in the existing session.

originSurfaceKind.description: Surface that originated the continuation request. Persisted caller session authority overrides this routing fact.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

clientId.description: Logical caller id. Defaults to omi-control-tools.

metadata.description: Small structured metadata for this run.

originSurfaceKind.description: Surface that originated the continuation request. Persisted caller session authority overrides this routing fact.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

clientId.description: Logical caller id. Defaults to omi-control-tools.

metadata.description: Small structured metadata for this run.

capabilityDoc.title: Send Agent Message
capabilityDoc.summary: Send a follow-up message to an existing canonical Omi agent session.
bullets: Use when continuing a multi-turn conversation with an Omi-managed agent by sessionId.
Creates a new run in the existing session.

runtimePreconditions: Defaults ownerId to the active signed-in owner when omitted.
Requires an existing sessionId from list_agent_sessions; cannot create a new session.
Rejects synchronous nested runs when the selected adapter is already executing for the session or has no capacity.

name: spawn_background_agent
label: Spawn Background Agent
description:
Internal Swift coordinator entrypoint for creating canonical floating-bar runs.

Not exposed to agent-facing surfaces.
promptSnippet: spawn_background_agent - Internal coordinator spawn
promptGuidelines: Swift coordinator entrypoint only.

originSurfaceKind.description: Surface that originated the spawn request. Persisted caller session authority overrides this routing fact.

surfaceKind.description: Optional session surface kind. Default floating_bar.

externalRefKind.description: Optional external reference kind for UI projection.

externalRefId.description: Optional external reference id for UI projection.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

clientId.description: Logical caller id. Defaults to omi-control-tools.

metadata.description: Small structured metadata for this run.

originSurfaceKind.description: Surface that originated the spawn request. Persisted caller session authority overrides this routing fact.

surfaceKind.description: Optional session surface kind. Default floating_bar.

externalRefKind.description: Optional external reference kind for UI projection.

externalRefId.description: Optional external reference id for UI projection.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

clientId.description: Logical caller id. Defaults to omi-control-tools.

metadata.description: Small structured metadata for this run.

capabilityDoc.title: Spawn Background Agent
capabilityDoc.summary: Internal Swift coordinator entrypoint for creating canonical floating-bar runs.
bullets: Swift coordinator entrypoint only; not advertised to agent-facing surfaces.

runtimePreconditions: Defaults ownerId to the active signed-in owner when omitted.
Creates a canonical floating_bar session/run by default.

name: spawn_agent
label: Spawn Agent
description:
Start canonical Omi background work. Visible runs project into floating-bar pills; invisible runs stay kernel-only child work.

Pass parentRunId to link the new run to a parent.
promptSnippet: spawn_agent - Start canonical Omi background work
promptGuidelines: Calling spawn_agent is the only way to start a visible floating-bar background agent; saying you will start one does not start it.
Use visible=false for parent-linked background work that should not appear as a pill.
If the user asks to use OpenClaw or Hermes, pass provider='openclaw' or provider='hermes'.
Inspect progress with list_agent_sessions or get_agent_run.

requestedAgentCount.description: Number of sibling agents requested in this single canonical route decision (default 1, maximum 8).

parentRunId.description: Optional parent run to link via delegation.

visible.description: Whether to project into floating-bar pill UI. Default true.

externalRefId.description: Optional stable pill id for UI projection.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

clientId.description: Logical caller id. Defaults to omi-control-tools.

metadata.description: Small structured metadata for this run.

requestedAgentCount.description: Number of sibling agents requested in this single canonical route decision (default 1, maximum 8).

parentRunId.description: Optional parent run to link via delegation.

visible.description: Whether to project into floating-bar pill UI. Default true.

externalRefId.description: Optional stable pill id for UI projection.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

clientId.description: Logical caller id. Defaults to omi-control-tools.

metadata.description: Small structured metadata for this run.

capabilityDoc.title: Spawn Agent
capabilityDoc.summary: Start canonical Omi background work and optionally project it into floating-bar pills.
bullets: Creates a canonical kernel session/run; visible runs project into floating-bar pills.

voice.realtimeDescription: Start canonical Omi background work. Visible runs appear as floating-bar pills. Use for multi-step work in the user's apps/browser/files that you cannot do directly.

parent_run_id.description: Optional parent run to link via delegation.

visible.description: Whether to project into floating-bar pill UI. Default true.

brief.description: Optional short user-visible summary for the floating pill.

runtimePreconditions: Defaults ownerId to the active signed-in owner when omitted.
With parentRunId, creates a delegated child session; without it, creates a new top-level background session.
Creates a canonical floating_bar session/run when visible=true.

name: run_agent_and_wait
label: Run Agent And Wait
description: Run a parent-linked child agent synchronously and return its structured result.
promptSnippet: run_agent_and_wait - Run a linked child agent and wait for the result
promptGuidelines: Use for synchronous structured child results linked to a known parent run.

objective.description: Delegated objective for the child agent.

originSurfaceKind.description: Surface that originated the synchronous delegation request. Persisted caller session authority overrides this routing fact.

context.description: Optional concise context, not a full transcript.

ownerId.description: Optional owner guard for the parent run.

clientId.description: Logical caller id. Defaults to omi-control-tools.

maxDepth.description: Maximum delegation depth for this call. Default 3, hard max 5.

maxBudgetUsd.description: Per-delegation budget guard. Default 5, hard max 10.

metadata.description: Small structured metadata for the child run.

objective.description: Delegated objective for the child agent.

originSurfaceKind.description: Surface that originated the synchronous delegation request. Persisted caller session authority overrides this routing fact.

context.description: Optional concise context, not a full transcript.

ownerId.description: Optional owner guard for the parent run.

clientId.description: Logical caller id. Defaults to omi-control-tools.

maxDepth.description: Maximum delegation depth for this call. Default 3, hard max 5.

maxBudgetUsd.description: Per-delegation budget guard. Default 5, hard max 10.

metadata.description: Small structured metadata for the child run.

capabilityDoc.title: Run Agent And Wait
capabilityDoc.summary: Run a parent-linked child agent synchronously and return its structured result.
bullets: Use for synchronous structured child results linked to a known parent run.

runtimePreconditions: Requires parentRunId.
Rejects synchronous nested runs when the selected adapter is already executing for the child session or has no capacity.

name: set_desktop_attention_override
label: Set Desktop Attention Override
description:
Dismiss or hide a kernel-derived attention subject such as a floating-bar run.

Pill dismissal writes here; it never deletes canonical run state.
promptSnippet: set_desktop_attention_override - Dismiss or hide a derived attention subject
promptGuidelines: Use dismissed=true to hide a floating-bar pill without deleting its canonical run.
Use subjectKind=run and subjectId=<runId> for pill dismissal.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

subjectKind.description: Attention subject kind, e.g. run or session.

dismissed.description: Whether the subject is dismissed. Default true.

ownerId.description: Owner id. Defaults to the active signed-in owner.

subjectKind.description: Attention subject kind, e.g. run or session.

dismissed.description: Whether the subject is dismissed. Default true.

capabilityDoc.title: Set Desktop Attention Override
capabilityDoc.summary: Dismiss or hide a kernel-derived attention subject such as a floating-bar run.
bullets: Use dismissed=true to hide floating-bar pills without deleting canonical run state.

runtimePreconditions: Defaults ownerId to the active signed-in owner when omitted.

name: search_tasks
label: Search Tasks
description: Vector similarity search on tasks. Find tasks by meaning or topic.
promptSnippet: search_tasks - Find tasks by meaning
runtimePreconditions: Requires local task index.

capabilityDoc.title: Search Tasks
capabilityDoc.summary: Vector similarity search on tasks (action_items + staged_tasks).
bullets: Use for finding tasks by meaning, not exact keywords, e.g. "find tasks about shopping".
Examples: "tasks about shopping", "anything related to the presentation".
Parameters: query (required), include_completed (default false).
More reliable than hand-writing MATCH queries for task search.

name: complete_task
label: Complete Task
description: Toggle a task's completion status. Syncs to backend.
promptSnippet: complete_task - Mark a task as complete/incomplete
runtimePreconditions: Requires a backendId found via execute_sql or search_tasks.

capabilityDoc.title: Complete Task
capabilityDoc.summary: Toggle a task's completion status by backendId.
bullets: Use after finding the task with execute_sql or search_tasks.

name: delete_task
label: Delete Task
description: Delete a task permanently. Syncs to backend.
promptSnippet: delete_task - Delete a task permanently
runtimePreconditions: Requires a backendId found via execute_sql or search_tasks.

capabilityDoc.title: Delete Task
capabilityDoc.summary: Delete a task permanently by backendId.
bullets: Use after finding the task with execute_sql or search_tasks.

name: load_skill
label: Load Skill
description: Load the full instructions for a named skill listed in available_skills.
promptSnippet: load_skill - Load the full SKILL.md instructions for an available skill

name.description: Skill name exactly as listed in available_skills

runtimePreconditions: Requires a local SKILL.md under the configured skill roots.

capabilityDoc.title: Load Skill
capabilityDoc.summary: Load the full instructions for a named skill listed in available_skills.
bullets: Use the exact skill name from available_skills.

name: save_knowledge_graph
label: Save Knowledge Graph
description: Save a knowledge graph of entities and relationships discovered about the user.
promptSnippet: save_knowledge_graph - Save entities and relationships to the user's knowledge graph
promptGuidelines: Use when exploring the user's files during onboarding or knowledge-graph building.
Deduplication is handled automatically; include all meaningful entities and relationships you found.

id.description: Stable node id, referenced by edges.

runtimePreconditions: Used by onboarding/knowledge graph flows.

capabilityDoc.title: Save Knowledge Graph
capabilityDoc.summary: Save a knowledge graph of entities and relationships extracted from the user's data.
bullets: Parameters: nodes (array of {id, label, node_type, aliases}), edges (array of {source_id, target_id, label}).
node_type must be one of: person, organization, place, thing, concept.
Use when exploring the user's files during onboarding to build their knowledge graph.
Deduplication is handled automatically; provide all entities you find.

name: get_conversations
label: Get Conversations
description: Retrieve user conversations with summaries, action items, metadata. Use for time-based queries or recaps.
promptSnippet: get_conversations - Retrieve conversations by date range
runtimePreconditions: Requires authenticated backend access.

capabilityDoc.title: Get Conversations
capabilityDoc.summary: Retrieve conversations by recency or date range.
bullets: Use for latest/recent conversations and time-based conversation retrieval.
For voice, this returns summaries only and should be spoken briefly.

voice.realtimeDescription: List the user's MOST RECENT conversations, newest first (titles + summaries, no full transcripts). Use this — NOT search_conversations — for 'what was my most recent / latest / last conversation', 'what did we just talk about', or 'my recent conversations'. search_conversations is semantic and does NOT order by time, so it's wrong for 'recent'. Fast synchronous read. Speak the result.

name: search_conversations
label: Search Conversations
description: Semantic search across conversations. Use for specific events or topics.
promptSnippet: search_conversations - Find conversations about a topic

query.description: Event or topic to search for

runtimePreconditions: Requires authenticated backend access.

capabilityDoc.title: Search Conversations
capabilityDoc.summary: Semantic search across the user's past conversations.
bullets: Use for specific topics, decisions, or events discussed in conversations.

voice.realtimeDescription: Search the user's past conversations for what they discussed ('what did I say about X', 'what did we decide', 'summarize my last meeting'). Returns titles + summaries only (no full transcripts). Fast synchronous read. Speak the result.

name: get_memories
label: Get Memories
description: Retrieve user memories - facts, preferences, habits. Use for 'what do you know about me?' type questions.
promptSnippet: get_memories - Retrieve stored facts and preferences
runtimePreconditions: Requires authenticated backend access.

capabilityDoc.title: Get Memories
capabilityDoc.summary: Retrieve stored facts, preferences, habits, people, and background about the user.
bullets: Use for broad 'what do you know about me' questions or personal facts.

voice.realtimeDescription: Read what Omi knows about the user — their memories and facts (preferences, background, people, habits). Fast synchronous read with NO query. Use this for 'who am I', 'what do you know about me', 'what are my preferences'. Speak what it returns.

name: search_memories
label: Search Memories
description: Semantic search across user memories. Find memories about a topic using AI embeddings.
promptSnippet: search_memories - Find memories about a topic
runtimePreconditions: Requires authenticated backend access.

capabilityDoc.title: Search Memories
capabilityDoc.summary: Semantic search across user memories.
bullets: Use for a specific personal fact that is not already in the visible user context.

voice.realtimeDescription: Search the user's memories / facts for a SPECIFIC thing ('what's my dog's name', 'where do I work', 'what's my partner's name'). Fast synchronous read. Speak the result.

name: get_action_items
label: Get Action Items
description: Retrieve user tasks from Omi backend. Filter by completion status or due date.
promptSnippet: get_action_items - Retrieve tasks
runtimePreconditions: Requires authenticated backend access.

capabilityDoc.title: Get Action Items
capabilityDoc.summary: Retrieve the user's tasks with optional completion and due-date filters.
bullets: Use for completed tasks, date ranges, or the full task list.
For voice, prefer get_tasks for plain overdue/due-today questions.

voice.realtimeDescription: Read the user's tasks / to-dos from the backend, with optional filters. Use for COMPLETED tasks ('what did I finish'), a DATE RANGE ('what's due next week'), or the FULL list ('all my tasks') — for plain 'what's due today / overdue', prefer get_tasks. Fast synchronous read. Speak a short summary of what it returns.

name: create_action_item
label: Create Action Item
description: Create a new task. Use when user explicitly asks to add a task.
promptSnippet: create_action_item - Create a new task
runtimePreconditions: Requires authenticated backend access.

capabilityDoc.title: Create Action Item
capabilityDoc.summary: Create a new task, to-do, or reminder.
bullets: Use when the user explicitly asks to add something to their list.
Pass a concise description and due_at only when the user gave a time.

voice.realtimeDescription: Create a new task / to-do / reminder for the user ('remind me to…', 'add … to my list', 'I need to…'). Fast synchronous write. Confirm out loud after it returns.

name: update_action_item
label: Update Action Item
description: Update task status, description, or due date.
promptSnippet: update_action_item - Update an existing task
runtimePreconditions: Requires authenticated backend access.

capabilityDoc.title: Update Action Item
capabilityDoc.summary: Update an existing task's status, description, or due date.
bullets: Find the task first, then update the matching id. Do not guess task ids.

voice.realtimeDescription: Update an existing task: mark it done, edit its text, or reschedule it. You MUST first call get_tasks to get the matching task's id, then pass that id here. Fast synchronous write.

completed.description: Set true to mark the task done.

description.description: New task text, if changing it.

due_at.description: New ISO-8601 due date/time, if rescheduling.

name: capture_screen
label: Capture Screen
description: Capture raw screenshot pixels only when get_work_context is insufficient. Returns the saved full-screen image path plus native-resolution detail tiles on large screens, after approval. Use the Read tool to view the images after capturing.
promptSnippet: capture_screen - Take a screenshot of the user's current screen
promptGuidelines: Call get_work_context first when the user asks about what's on their screen or what they're looking at.
Use capture_screen only when raw pixels are necessary; it requires explicit approval before image bytes are shared.
After capture_screen returns, use Read to view the full-screen image.

The full screenshot is downscaled before you see it — before quoting small on-screen text (titles, prices, sizes, labels) or choosing between similar-looking items, Read the detail tile covering that item and take the exact text from the tile.
Keep every detail you cite (title, price, badge, position) bound to one on-screen item; if text is not legible even in a tile, say so instead of inferring.
Do NOT use bash screencapture - always use this tool instead.
runtimePreconditions: Requires macOS Screen Recording permission.

capabilityDoc.title: Capture Screen
capabilityDoc.summary: Capture raw screenshot pixels after screen summary context is not enough.
bullets: For screen-awareness questions, call get_work_context first.
Use capture_screen only when raw pixels are necessary; it requires explicit approval before image bytes are shared.
The result lists the full-screen image path plus native-resolution detail tiles on large screens; use Read to view them.

name: check_permission_status
label: Check Permission Status
description: Check whether a required macOS permission has been granted. Use before requesting access, or after a permission request.
promptSnippet: check_permission_status - Check macOS permission status

type.description: Optional permission type. Omit to return all supported permissions.

runtimePreconditions: Requires local desktop app.

capabilityDoc.title: Check Permission Status
capabilityDoc.summary: Check whether a required macOS permission has been granted.
bullets: Use before requesting a permission or after request_permission returns pending.
Omit type to check all supported permissions.

voice.realtimeDescription: Check whether Omi has the requested macOS permission through the kernel-authorized native executor.

name: request_permission
label: Request Permission
description: Open the native macOS permission prompt or Settings pane for one required permission after the user explicitly asks for it. Screen share, screen sharing, and screen-share mean screen_recording.
promptSnippet: request_permission - Request a macOS permission
promptGuidelines: Call only when the current user message explicitly requests one named permission, clearly affirms your immediately preceding one-permission request, or directly says to request it/that permission.
Treat screen share, screen sharing, and screen-share as the screen_recording permission type.
For generic or multi-permission requests, ask the user which permission they want to grant.
Use strict permission types only. Do not invent permission names.
After requesting, explain any returned requires_restart or pending status.

type.description: Permission type: screen_recording, microphone, notifications, accessibility, automation, or full_disk_access

runtimePreconditions: Requires explicit current-turn user consent; some macOS permissions require the user to toggle Settings manually.

capabilityDoc.title: Request Permission
capabilityDoc.summary: Open or guide the user through granting a required macOS permission. Screen sharing is the macOS Screen Recording permission.
bullets: Call only when the current user message names one permission, clearly affirms your immediately preceding one-permission request, or directly says to request it/that permission.
Treat screen share, screen sharing, and screen-share as the screen_recording permission type.
Ask the user to choose when their request is generic or names multiple permissions.
The user must still complete the native macOS prompt or Settings toggle.

voice.realtimeDescription: Request Omi's macOS permission through the kernel-authorized native executor by opening the native prompt or relevant System Settings pane. Screen share, screen sharing, and screen-share mean Screen Recording. Supports Screen Recording, microphone, notifications, Accessibility, Automation, and Full Disk Access.

name: scan_files
label: Scan Files
description: Scan selected files/folders during onboarding to build local context.
promptSnippet: scan_files - Scan files for onboarding context
runtimePreconditions: Onboarding-only.

capabilityDoc.title: Scan Files
capabilityDoc.summary: Scan selected files/folders during onboarding to build local context.
bullets: Onboarding-only.

name: set_user_preferences
label: Set User Preferences
description: Persist onboarding preferences such as name and language.
promptSnippet: set_user_preferences - Save onboarding preferences
runtimePreconditions: Onboarding-only.

capabilityDoc.title: Set User Preferences
capabilityDoc.summary: Persist onboarding preferences such as name and language.
bullets: Onboarding-only.

name: ask_followup
label: Ask Followup
description: Ask the user a follow-up onboarding question with optional quick replies.
promptSnippet: ask_followup - Ask an onboarding follow-up question

question.description: The question to present to the user

options.description: 2-3 quick-reply button labels. For permissions, include 'Grant [Permission]' and 'Skip'.

runtimePreconditions: Onboarding-only.

capabilityDoc.title: Ask Followup
capabilityDoc.summary: Ask the user a follow-up onboarding question with optional quick replies.
bullets: Onboarding-only.

name: complete_onboarding
label: Complete Onboarding
description: Complete onboarding after required goals and context are collected.
promptSnippet: complete_onboarding - Complete onboarding
runtimePreconditions: Onboarding-only.

capabilityDoc.title: Complete Onboarding
capabilityDoc.summary: Complete onboarding after required goals and context are collected.
bullets: Onboarding-only.

name: get_email_insights
label: Get Email Insights
description: Read precomputed email/calendar onboarding insights.
promptSnippet: get_email_insights - Read onboarding email/calendar insights
runtimePreconditions: Onboarding-only; requires background insights to be loaded.

capabilityDoc.title: Get Email Insights
capabilityDoc.summary: Read precomputed email/calendar onboarding insights.
bullets: Onboarding-only; requires background insights to be loaded.

name: get_tasks
label: Get Tasks
description: Read the user's overdue and due-today tasks locally for voice responses.
promptSnippet: get_tasks - Read overdue and due-today tasks locally
runtimePreconditions: Realtime voice only; requires local TasksStore.

capabilityDoc.title: Get Tasks
capabilityDoc.summary: Read the user's overdue and due-today tasks locally.
bullets: Use for plain voice questions like what are my tasks, what's due today, or what's on my list.
Prefer get_action_items for completed tasks, date ranges, or the full list.

voice.realtimeDescription: Read the user's tasks (overdue + due today) locally and get them back as text to speak. Fast synchronous read — use this for 'what are my tasks', 'what's due today', 'what's on my list'. Reading tasks is always a direct call, never background work.

name: create_calendar_event
label: Create Calendar Event
description: Create a Google Calendar event through the backend calendar tool.
promptSnippet: create_calendar_event - Create a Google Calendar event

start_time.description: Event start time in ISO-8601 with timezone.

end_time.description: Event end time in ISO-8601 with timezone.

attendees.description: Optional comma-separated attendee names or email addresses.

runtimePreconditions: Requires authenticated backend calendar access.

capabilityDoc.title: Create Calendar Event
capabilityDoc.summary: Create a new Google Calendar event.
bullets: Use when the user asks to add, create, schedule, or put a specific event on their calendar.
Pass title, start_time, and end_time as ISO-8601 strings with timezone; include location, description, and attendees when provided.
This capability creates one specified event; it does not find availability, reschedule, delete, or coordinate with people.

voice.realtimeDescription: Create one specified Google Calendar event. Requires start_time and end_time as ISO-8601 strings with timezone. This capability does not find availability, reschedule, delete, or coordinate with people.

start_time.description: Event start time in ISO-8601 with timezone, e.g. 2026-06-28T14:00:00-04:00.

end_time.description: Event end time in ISO-8601 with timezone, e.g. 2026-06-28T15:00:00-04:00.

attendees.description: Optional comma-separated attendee names or email addresses.

name: ask_higher_model
label: Ask Higher Model
description: Escalate a hard question to the larger model and speak its answer.
promptSnippet: ask_higher_model - Escalate to a higher model for a second opinion

context.description: Optional relevant context for the escalation.

runtimePreconditions: Realtime voice only.

capabilityDoc.title: Ask Higher Model
capabilityDoc.summary: Get a second opinion from the larger model when the user pushes back or current facts are needed.
bullets: Use sparingly; answer simple or creative requests yourself.

voice.realtimeDescription: Get a second opinion from a smarter model and receive text to speak. Use it when the user is dissatisfied with your previous answer (pushes back, rephrases, says you're wrong, or asks for a better/deeper answer), or when you genuinely need precise up-to-date facts you don't know. Answer general, creative, and long-form requests yourself.

context.description: Relevant context you already have that helps answer well — facts you fetched, what the user is referring to, or the previous answer they pushed back on. Include only what's relevant; omit if there's nothing useful.

name: screenshot
label: Screenshot
description: Capture the user's current screen for realtime vision.
promptSnippet: screenshot - Capture the user's current screen
runtimePreconditions: Realtime voice only; requires Screen Recording permission.

capabilityDoc.title: Screenshot
capabilityDoc.summary: Capture the user's current screen.
bullets: Use when the user asks about what is on screen.

voice.realtimeDescription: Capture the user's current screen so you can see what they're looking at.

name: point_click
label: Point Click
description: Click at on-screen pixel coordinates.
promptSnippet: point_click - Click at on-screen coordinates
runtimePreconditions: Realtime voice only; requires Accessibility permission.

capabilityDoc.title: Point Click
capabilityDoc.summary: Click at on-screen pixel coordinates.
bullets: Use only when the user clearly asks you to click something.

voice.realtimeDescription: Click the mouse at on-screen pixel coordinates.

name: get_local_status
label: Get Local Status
description: Report whether local Omi Desktop context is available, including screen-history counts, indexed screenshot counts, and latest capture time.
promptSnippet: get_local_status - Check local desktop context status
runtimePreconditions: Local API only.

capabilityDoc.title: Get Local Status
capabilityDoc.summary: Report whether local Omi Desktop context is available.
bullets: Local API only.

name: get_screenshot
label: Get Screenshot
description: Fetch a local Rewind screenshot image by screenshot_id.
promptSnippet: get_screenshot - Fetch a local screenshot image

screenshot_id.description: Screenshot ID from search_screen_history or screenshots table

runtimePreconditions: Local API only.

capabilityDoc.title: Get Screenshot
capabilityDoc.summary: Fetch a local Rewind screenshot image by screenshot_id.
bullets: Local API only.

name: get_work_context
label: Get Work Context
description: Get the user's current screen plus a compressed timeline of recent on-screen activity without sharing raw screenshot pixels. Call this first when seeing the user's current work would help.
promptSnippet: get_work_context - Get current screen and recent work context
promptGuidelines: Call get_work_context first for "what is on my screen", "do you see my screen", and current-work questions.
Use its screen_now and timeline fields to answer directly when possible.
Only request get_screenshot or capture_screen approval if raw image pixels are necessary after get_work_context.

minutes.description: Minutes of recent activity to summarize (default 10, max 120)

runtimePreconditions: Requires local Rewind database; raw screenshot pixels still require separate approval.

capabilityDoc.title: Get Work Context
capabilityDoc.summary: Get the user's current screen plus a compressed timeline of recent on-screen activity.
bullets: Call this first for "what is on my screen", "do you see my screen", and current-work questions.
Returns availability, a screenshot_id for follow-up, OCR preview, and recent timeline without raw image bytes.
If raw pixels are needed after this, request get_screenshot/capture_screen approval.
